ogTitle: North Burngup vs North Cascade: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Burngup and North Cascade.
heading: North Burngup vs North Cascade — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Burngup, Western Australia and North Cascade, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Cascade has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to North Burngup (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Burngup or North Cascade safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Burngup has a flood risk score of 37/100, while North Cascade scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Burngup scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. North Cascade scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Burngup or North Cascade?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Cascade has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
